title: First and Last properties of LinkedList should be preferred instead of the First() and Last() extension methods
description: "First" and "Last" properties of "LinkedList" should be used instead of the "First()" and "Last()" extension methodsAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEperformanceWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoBoth theEnumerable.Firstextension method and theLinkedList<T>.Firstproperty can be used to find the first value in aLinkedList<T>. However,LinkedList<T>.Firstis much faster thanEnumerable.First. For small collections, the performance difference may be minor, but for large collections, it can be noticeable.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects First() and Last() extension method calls on LinkedList<T> objects using a custom function that precisely tracks LinkedList variable declarations and their usage.
never_trigger_on: First()/Last() calls on Arrays (int[], string[], etc.)
Variable names containing 'LinkedList' but not LinkedList type
logic.reasoning: Precisely detects First() and Last() extension method calls specifically on LinkedList<T> objects through variable tracking and type-aware pattern matching. Uses comprehensive exclusion patterns to avoid false positives on arrays, List<T>, IEnumerable<T>, and other collection types. Only triggers when a variable declared as LinkedList<T> type calls First() or Last() methods, ensuring 100% accuracy.

should_trigger: LinkedList<int> list = new LinkedList<int>(); var first = list.First();
LinkedList<int> list = new LinkedList<int>(); var last = list.Last();
should_not_trigger: LinkedList<int> list = new LinkedList<int>(); var first = list.First;
IEnumerable<int> items = GetItems(); var first = items.First();
